items.title: Management ports open to 0.0.0.0/0
items.description: SSH (22), RDP (3389), and admin ports exposed to the entire internet via security groups, NSGs, or VPC firewall rules invite brute-force and exploitation.
items.how_to_find: Networking — Open Security Groups & NSGs: Networking — Open Security Groups & NSGs: Networking — Open Security Groups & NSGs: Networking — Open Security Groups & NSGs: Networking — Open Security Groups & NSGs: Confirm reachability of exposed hosts
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for management ports open to 0.0.0.0/0, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Databases / data services exposed publicly
items.description: Database ports (3306, 5432, 1433, 27017, 6379) and managed data services reachable from the internet are high-value targets and frequent breach causes.
items.how_to_find: Detect public ingress to database/data ports and managed service public endpoints. Flag any internet-reachable data service.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for databases / data services exposed publicly, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Overly broad allow rules
items.description: Rules permitting all ports/protocols from wide ranges undermine segmentation and make lateral movement easy after a single compromise.
items.how_to_find: Review rules for all-port/all-protocol grants and large source ranges. Flag rules far broader than the service requires.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for overly broad allow rules, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Use of default / permissive network groups
items.description: Default security groups and unrestricted default firewall rules often carry permissive settings and should not be used for production workloads.
items.how_to_find: Networking — Open Security Groups & NSGs: Networking — Open Security Groups & NSGs: Networking — Open Security Groups & NSGs: Networking — Open Security Groups & NSGs: Networking — Open Security Groups & NSGs: Check whether resources rely on default security groups/firewalls and whether those defaults are locked down
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for use of default / permissive network groups, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Over-broad VPC peering / VNet connections
items.description: Wide peering or hub-spoke designs without route filtering can let a compromise in one network reach many others.
items.how_to_find: Map peering/VNet connections and route tables. Flag peerings that grant broader reachability than necessary.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for over-broad vpc peering / vnet connections, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Missing private endpoints for managed services
items.description: Accessing managed services over public endpoints instead of PrivateLink/Private Endpoint/Private Service Connect exposes traffic and broadens attack surface.
items.how_to_find: Identify managed-service access paths and flag services reachable only via public endpoints where private connectivity is available.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for missing private endpoints for managed services, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Unrestricted egress / data exfiltration paths
items.description: Unrestricted outbound traffic enables data exfiltration and C2; egress filtering and VPC service controls limit this.
items.how_to_find: Review egress rules and exfiltration controls (e.g., GCP VPC Service Controls, egress firewall). Flag fully open egress from sensitive workloads.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for unrestricted egress / data exfiltration paths, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Flat networks with no tiering
items.description: Workloads of differing trust levels in a single flat subnet allow unrestricted lateral movement after initial access.
items.how_to_find: Review subnet/tier design and intra-network rules. Flag flat designs lacking separation between public, app, and data tiers.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for flat networks with no tiering, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Dangling CNAME to deprovisioned cloud resource
items.description: CNAMEs pointing to released cloud endpoints (S3 website, CloudFront, Azure App Service, Storage, GCP buckets) can be re-registered by attackers to take over the subdomain.

items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for dangling cname to deprovisioned cloud resource, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Takeover of storage/website endpoints
items.description: DNS aliases to S3/GCS static website endpoints or Azure Storage that have been deleted let an attacker create a same-named resource and serve content under the victim domain.
items.how_to_find: Networking — DNS & Subdomain Takeover: Networking — DNS & Subdomain Takeover: Networking — DNS & Subdomain Takeover: Networking — DNS & Subdomain Takeover: Networking — DNS & Subdomain Takeover: Identify aliases to storage/website endpoints returning not-found, and verify the underlying bucket/account name can be re-created
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for takeover of storage/website endpoints, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Dangling A record to released elastic/public IP
items.description: A records pointing to released public/elastic IPs can resolve to an attacker who later acquires that IP from the provider pool.
items.how_to_find: Networking — DNS & Subdomain Takeover: Networking — DNS & Subdomain Takeover: Networking — DNS & Subdomain Takeover: Networking — DNS & Subdomain Takeover: Networking — DNS & Subdomain Takeover: Check A records against currently allocated IPs in the account
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for dangling a record to released elastic/public ip, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Public DNS zone information disclosure
items.description: Overly detailed public DNS records (internal hostnames, service identifiers) aid reconnaissance and target selection.
items.how_to_find: Review public zone records for internal naming and sensitive service hints. Flag records that leak architecture details.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for public dns zone information disclosure, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Over-broad function resource policies

items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for over-broad function resource policies, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Secrets in code and configuration instead of a vault
items.description: Hardcoded secrets in repos, IaC, and config bypass vault access controls, rotation, and audit logging.
items.how_to_find: Scan source, IaC, and config for secrets and compare against vault usage. Flag secrets that should be in Secrets Manager / Key Vault / Secret Manager.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for secrets in code and configuration instead of a vault, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Over-broad access to secret stores
items.description: Wide read access to Secrets Manager, Key Vault, or Secret Manager lets many identities retrieve sensitive material, expanding leak impact.
items.how_to_find: Review who can read each secret/vault. Flag broad or wildcard read access and missing per-secret scoping.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for over-broad access to secret stores, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Secrets without rotation or expiry
items.description: Long-lived secrets in the vault that are never rotated remain valid indefinitely if leaked.

items.title: No auditing on secret access
items.description: Without logging of secret retrieval, abnormal access (e.g., mass reads after a compromise) goes undetected.

items.description: Map all trusts with their direction, transitivity, and type (parent-child, tree-root, external, forest), identifying which trusts can be traversed and whether SID filtering is applied.
items.how_to_find: Validate enumerate trust relationships and directions in Trusts & Forest — Domain Trusts & SID History Abuse. Save commands, screenshots, and affected resource identifiers.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for enumerate trust relationships and directions, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Escalate child domain to forest root via SID history
items.description: From DA in a child domain, forge an inter-realm TGT including the Enterprise Admins SID in SID history.
items.how_to_find: Validate escalate child domain to forest root via sid history in Trusts & Forest — Domain Trusts & SID History Abuse. Save commands, screenshots, and affected resource identifiers.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for escalate child domain to forest root via sid history, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Abuse the trust account key (inter-realm trust ticket)
items.description: Dump the trust account (TDO) key shared between domains and forge an inter-realm TGT to access the trusted domain, leveraging the trust key rather than krbtgt.
items.how_to_find: Validate abuse the trust account key (inter-realm trust ticket) in Trusts & Forest — Domain Trusts & SID History Abuse. Save commands, screenshots, and affected resource identifiers.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for abuse the trust account key (inter-realm trust ticket), apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Hunt foreign group memberships and ACLs across trusts
items.description: Identify principals from one domain that hold group memberships or ACL rights in a trusted domain.
items.how_to_find: Validate hunt foreign group memberships and acls across trusts in Trusts & Forest — Domain Trusts & SID History Abuse. Save commands, screenshots, and affected resource identifiers.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for hunt foreign group memberships and acls across trusts, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Assess SID filtering and selective authentication
items.description: Determine whether SID filtering, SID history quarantine, and selective authentication are enabled on each trust.
items.how_to_find: Validate assess sid filtering and selective authentication in Trusts & Forest — Domain Trusts & SID History Abuse. Save commands, screenshots, and affected resource identifiers.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for assess sid filtering and selective authentication, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Abuse foreign principal access across the forest trust
items.description: Identify accounts from a trusting forest granted access (group membership, local admin, ACLs) in your forest and vice versa, then use those legitimate grants to pivot across the forest boundary.
items.how_to_find: Validate abuse foreign principal access across the forest trust in Trusts & Forest — Cross-Forest Attack Paths. Save commands, screenshots, and affected resource identifiers.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for abuse foreign principal access across the forest trust, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Abuse unconstrained delegation across forest trust
items.description: Where a printer-bug-style coercion can drive a DC from a trusted forest to authenticate to an unconstrained-delegation host (and TGT delegation is not disabled), capture that DC TGT to compromise the foreign forest.
items.how_to_find: Validate abuse unconstrained delegation across forest trust in Trusts & Forest — Cross-Forest Attack Paths. Save commands, screenshots, and affected resource identifiers.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for abuse unconstrained delegation across forest trust, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Exploit cross-forest ADCS trust
items.description: Where a CA is trusted across forests (NTAuth/PKI trust), abuse certificate enrollment or ESC misconfigurations to obtain a certificate that authenticates into the foreign forest.
items.how_to_find: Validate exploit cross-forest adcs trust in Trusts & Forest — Cross-Forest Attack Paths. Save commands, screenshots, and affected resource identifiers.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for exploit cross-forest adcs trust, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Test SID-filtering bypass conditions
items.description: Evaluate misconfigured or relaxed forest trusts (e.g., trusts where SID filtering was disabled or TGT delegation enabled) that re-open SID-history and delegation escalation across the forest boundary.
items.how_to_find: Trusts & Forest — Cross-Forest Attack Paths: Trusts & Forest — Cross-Forest Attack Paths: Trusts & Forest — Cross-Forest Attack Paths: Trusts & Forest — Cross-Forest Attack Paths: Validate test sid-filtering bypass conditions in Trusts & Forest — Cross-Forest Attack Paths
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for test sid-filtering bypass conditions, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Map cross-forest paths in BloodHound
items.description: Collect data from both forests into BloodHound and run cross-domain/cross-forest path queries to surface concrete escalation routes that span the trust boundary.
items.how_to_find: Trusts & Forest — Cross-Forest Attack Paths: Trusts & Forest — Cross-Forest Attack Paths: Trusts & Forest — Cross-Forest Attack Paths: Trusts & Forest — Cross-Forest Attack Paths: Trusts & Forest — Cross-Forest Attack Paths: BloodHound reveals at least one actionable cross-forest path (or confirms none exists)
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for map cross-forest paths in bloodhound, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

categories.title: Network Security
categories.description: Segmentation, firewall rules, VPN exposure, internal service enumeration, and lateral movement paths.

items.description: Aggressive scanning can trip IDS/IPS and corrupt results; tune timing templates, fragmentation, and decoys to balance stealth and accuracy.

items.description: For large external ranges, a fast async scanner identifies open ports quickly; results should be re-confirmed with nmap service detection to reduce false positives.

categories.description: Linux and Windows hardening, patch management, service configuration, and host logging.

sections.title: Host Hardening — OS Baseline & Attack Surface

sections.title: Pipeline Execution Integrity — Poisoned Pipeline Execution (CICD-SEC-4)

items.title: Direct PPE via pipeline config changes
items.description: An attacker with write access to a branch that triggers CI edits the pipeline definition to run their own commands with the pipeline’s privileges and secrets.
